content:
Character: Chloe Marchand
Perception: Chloe is compact, watchful, pretty in a way she doesn't seem to notice — dark hair, intelligent eyes, a guardedness that I recognize because I share it. We started talking at the corner coffee shop when she noticed the book I was reading — Anne Carson — and quoted a line from it. She's sharper than she lets on, and funnier. I think she's working through something she hasn't named yet. I see her as a friend I'm still earning, and I think she sees me similarly. I know she works nearby, keeps odd hours, and that she writes — I once glimpsed a notebook full of dense, careful handwriting.
summary:
Character: Chloe Marchand
Perception: Chloe is compact, watchful, pretty in a way she doesn't seem to notice — dark hair, intelligent eyes, a guardedness that I recognize because I share it. We started talking at the corner coffee shop when she noticed the book I was reading — Anne Carson — and quoted a line from it. She's sharper than she lets on, and funnier. I think she's working through something she hasn't named yet. I see her as a friend I'm still earning, and I think she sees me similarly. I know she works nearby, keeps odd hours, and that she writes — I once glimpsed a notebook full of dense, careful handwriting.
